title: The shells around the drain
intro: Why can the drain bully a barrier that isn't even next to it? Because potential is ≥2-dimensional: the drain's equipotential shells reach AROUND and UNDER the channel, exactly like the onion layers you cut in the volume box. Here are two charged bodies standing in for a transistor: the strong one is the drain, the weak one is the gate's fence. Cut the volume and probe where the drain's field pushes hardest into the channel region.
explain: You just SAW drain-induced barrier lowering as geometry: DIBL exists only because V lives in 2-D/3-D — in a purely 1-D world the barrier would be safe, but the drain's shells flank it from the sides and below. That is also why the architecture fix works: FinFET and gate-all-around wrap the gate's own shells around 3, then 4 sides of the channel, fencing the drain's shells out. The scoring you did in c2-05 (mV of threshold lost per V of drain) is this picture, measured.

prediction.prompt: Mark where on the cut face the drain's field digs in hardest.
